---
title: "Math Nomad"
page-layout: full
toc: false
title-block-banner: false
body-classes: mn-home-page
listing:
  - id: mn-home-latest
    contents:
      - courses/resources
      - writing/articles
      - videos/entries
      - projects/entries
    sort: "date desc"
    type: default
    max-items: 6
    fields: [image, title, description, date]
    sort-ui: false
    filter-ui: false
    feed:
      items: 20
      type: partial
      title: "Latest from Math Nomad"
---

::: {.mn-hero}
::: {.mn-hero-copy}
# Mathematics worth returning to.

Math Nomad is a growing home for course materials, mathematical exposition, videos and projects in progress—written for curious readers at several stages of their journey.

::: {.mn-actions}
[Browse the writing](writing/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg}
[See current projects](projects/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-dark .btn-lg}
:::
:::

::: {.mn-hero-art}
<img class="img-fluid" src="assets/kolam-field.svg" alt="A field of sixteen square kolam tiles, each with a different pattern of openings.">
:::
:::

::: {.mn-feature-band}
::: {.content-block .mn-feature-grid}
::: {.mn-feature-copy}
<p class="mn-kicker">FEATURED INVESTIGATION</p>

## From sixteen tiles to fifty-one kolams

Every four-bit word encodes a square tile. Arrange all sixteen so that neighbouring edges match and the boundary closes, with the nonzero tiles forming one connected network. Then classify the accepted boards up to the symmetries of the square.

[Read the exposition](writing/articles/binary-kolam-tiles/index.qmd){.btn .btn-light .btn-lg}
[Visit the project](projects/entries/kolam-tile-laboratory/index.qmd){.mn-text-link}
:::

::: {.mn-feature-facts}
<div><strong>16</strong><span>binary tiles</span></div>
<div><strong>408</strong><span>accepted boards</span></div>
<div><strong>51</strong><span>symmetry classes</span></div>
:::
:::
:::

::: {.mn-intro .content-block}
## Find your way

Follow a course, settle into an essay, watch an idea develop, or look over the shoulder of a project still being made.
:::

::: {.mn-four-up .content-block}
::: {.mn-path-card}
### Courses

Curated pathways through notes, interactives, exercises and further reading.

[Visit the courses →](courses/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.mn-path-card}
### Writing

Research, exposition, outreach and teaching ideas for readers at different levels.

[Browse the writing →](writing/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.mn-path-card}
### Videos

YouTube videos accompanied by context, references and a longer account of the mathematics.

[Watch and read →](videos/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.mn-path-card}
### Projects

Active investigations, experiments and longer pieces of work, with updates as they develop.

[See what is in progress →](projects/index.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.content-block .mn-listing-section .mn-home-latest}
<p class="mn-kicker">RECENTLY PUBLISHED</p>

## Latest from Math Nomad

New course resources, articles, video companions and project updates appear here in publication order.

::: {#mn-home-latest}
:::
:::

::: {.mn-follow-band}
::: {.content-block .mn-follow-grid}
::: {.mn-follow-copy}
<p class="mn-kicker">RETURN WHEN THERE IS SOMETHING NEW</p>

## Follow at a human pace

Use the [RSS feed](index.xml) for every new publication. A quarterly email digest featuring a small selection of writing, resources and project updates is planned.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="mn-digest-panel">
  <p class="mn-kicker">QUARTERLY DIGEST</p>
  <h3>In preparation</h3>
  <p>A short selection is planned three or four times a year. Until sign-up is ready, RSS is the dependable way to follow every publication.</p>
  <a class="btn btn-primary" href="index.xml">Follow via RSS</a>
</div>
```
:::
:::
